feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Cloud Identity Services
feed.subtitle: SAP Cloud Identity Services Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: SAP Joule - Identity Provisioning - No assigned Roles displayed
entry.content:
Dear Community,

Over the past few days, we have set up SAP Joule in our Public Cloud environment. This has worked well. But now we have a problem with the assigned roles in identity provisioning. When I search for a user, I get the message that there are now assigned roles for each user.

The problem now is that Joule doesn't show any results when searching for a specific app or anything else because the rules or permissions are missing. Surely the users in the public cloud have the specific roles assigned.

As additional information, we have an IAS and an IPS tenant in Cloud Identity Services.

Has anyone had the same problem so far?

Thanks for your help in advance.

Greetings Tobias

Joule SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition SAP Cloud Identity Services

entry.title: Process Automation Rest Based Action on Identity Directory API
entry.content:
Hello Team,

I wanted to create an action in SAP Build Process Automation using the Identity Directory API from SAP Cloud Identity Service

But I am getting this error

Have anyone encountered the same? and how did you solve it?

 

Thank you in Advance! 

Alden

entry.title: IAS + ONB (Welcome Email Day 1)
entry.content:
Hi folks!

We have in place the onboardees login managed by IAS including the Provisioning Real Time Sync. So at the moment that the Onboarding Process starts the onboardee is created at IAS Platform. IAS is sending the Welcome Emails and they are able to login to access, so all good.

Question is:

Is there any way to send an additional welcome email once the onboardee becomes into an employee?

Once the onboardee is created into IAS, it has a IAS User Profile and it receives a Welcome Email thanks to:

 

{
   "condition": "$.userType != 'ONBOARDEE'",
   "targetPath": "$.emailTemplateSetId",
   "constant": "943fec28-aaaa-bbbb-cccc-aaee814453c6",
   "scope": "createEntity"
},
{
   "condition": "$.userType != 'ONBOARDEE'",
   "targetPath": "$.sendMail",
   "constant": "true",
   "scope": "createEntity"
},
{
   "condition": "$.userType == 'ONBOARDEE'",
   "targetPath": "$.emailTemplateSetId",
   "constant": "943fec28-xxxx-yyyy-zzzz-aaee814453c6",
   "scope": "createEntity"
},
{
   "condition": "$.userType == 'ONBOARDEE'",
   "targetPath": "$.sendMail",
   "constant": "true",
   "scope": "createEntity"
},

 

So during the "CreateEntity" the email is sent.

The thing is that during the Hiring Process, HR changes the onboardee data like the "username" and the "email" and those actions are replicated to the IAS User Profile (expected behaviour)

Our concerns is that at the moment the onboardee becomes into a employee, there is not another "CreateEntity" event (we can add the transformation, but doesn't work) it's only an UPDATE into the IAS User Profile, so the user (now is an employee) doesn't know the new username or email due to it was changed during the Hiring Process and is not the original one that he/she received as onboardee.

Is there any way to detect when someone change at IAS side from onboardee to employee in order to send another Welcome Emails with the current username value?

Regards,

Jose Alberto Vargas

entry.title: No Identity Provisioning on IAS
entry.content:
I am using an existing Internal IAS that I did not set up by request and it has no Identity Provisioning.
The"Manage Identity Provisioning" role is missing is not available or listed for Admins in the IAS Admin console.

Does it need to be bundled with IAS when initially requested?
From my understanding it is now auto included with IAS.

This is no help 3253894 - Identity Authentication Service "Manage Identity Provisioning" role is missing 
I am on a CF BTP Sub account and want to better understand with the demise of SAML2 on sub accounts for Trust to IAS.

entry.title: how do we automate space & org members access in SAP BTP cockpit(Cloud foundry env)
entry.content: needs options to automate BTP space & org member access either using some tools or in a different way to reduce manual intervention every time.

entry.title: User Content
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

We want to use SAP IPS for user provisioning to Cloud Apps (SAC, Enable Now...)

What happens to the content of users when these users are automatically deleted (e.g., in SAC) via IPS in these apps?

Thank you in advance.

Best Regards

entry.title: IPS - SCIM API V2 filter users by user group assignment
entry.content:
Hi, we have a requirement to filter out users in the IPS sync that belongs to a specific User Group in SF.

e.g.: users assigned to user group "GG-IASSYNC" should NOT sync to IAS.

In the Source system properties, we set up sf.group.filter with value displayName ne "GG-IASSYNC".

While in the Target transformation we added this condition: && ($.groups[?(@.displayName == 'GG-IASSYNC')] EMPTY true)

We are using sf.api.version 2, but the condition above does not seem to work.

How can we write that condition or how can we filter out ousers based on their user group?

Thanks

Marco

entry.title: SAP Identity Directory API not working from RAP & CAP
entry.content:
Hello,

We have a requirement to create Users in SAP BTP whenever the supplier registers with email id. For this SAP has provided Identity Directory API Overview | Identity Directory API | SAP Business Accelerator Hub

In above link I want to use Users.

Below is the destination set up

We are trying to call API from ABAP on Cloud and CAPM but it is failing in both cases below are the details.

From ABAP on Cloud:

 

When trying option 1 and 2 we are getting below error.

"401","detail":"Authentication credentials are invalid or not allowed for this endpoint."

CAPM:

After deploying to cloud foundry we get below error.

 

We get below error.

 

Surprisingly when we try from Postman hub it is working fine.

 Can anyone help?

entry.title: SAP BUILD Apps: Data item in repeat issue for REST API
entry.content:
Hello, I'm trying to do a simple list view in a Build Apps that calls data from a REST API (Identity Directory API). So far I've been able to successfully call the destination in Build Apps and receive data. I've also "Autodect fields from response" to set the Schema. But, I'm having an issue getting to a specific field in the response that is a Text/Number. 

 

I'm trying to get to items in the Resources Object list. But it's saying it's incompatible (why can't I drill deeper to compatible data types?) .

 

 

Is it possible that Build Apps is unable to bind to deep nested structures? 

 

Any advice would be greatly appreciated.

entry.title: Allowing SAP Support to logon to BTP services when Corp IDP is used
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We have established trust between our BTP subaccount/services and Cloud Identity Services and we are using our Corp IDP as the default Authenticating Identity Provider for the applications. We need to allow SAP support agents to logon to our BTP applications for support purposes. However, our internal Information Security team do not allow the creation of generic Corp IDP credentials. Generic accounts would also cause complexities with MFA as well.

For SAML 2.0 applications (e.g. Datasphere, SAC), we can enable the "Allow users stored in Identity Authentication service to log on", which generates an additional URL for IAS logon. SAP support agents can then use generic credentials from the local Identity Directory to logon. However, this option is not supported for applications using Open ID Connect.

I am aware we can enable Conditional Authentication against the application or enable "Available for User Logon" against the Default IDP (sap.default) in the subaccount. The problem which both of these approaches is it will change the UX for all users, as the user will either have to choose their authentication method (IAS or Default), or enter their email twice. This is OK in non-production, but not acceptable for Production.

 
Are there any other customers facing a similar scenario? Does anybody have any recommendations?
 
Ideally we'd like "Allow users stored in Identity Authentication service to log on" to be available for Open ID as well. This has been raised in the influence portal already for "long term consideration": https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/318381

Thanks

Andrew

entry.title: SAP Cloud Identity Services instantly redirects to Microsoft login
entry.content:
Hi

We are currently working on implementing authentication through the SAP Cloud Identity Services (CIS) in our custom application.

In CIS, I have added all users, assigned the correct roles to each user, and created groups for conditional authentication. We have users that authorize against CIS with a CIS user. We also have users that authorize against CIS through Microsoft Entra ID. These users are added to an application that uses OpenID Connect (OIDC).

Everything is fine until this point; it is now also possible to login into the custom application through CIS.

However, the problem arises when logging out a user that used their Microsoft Entra account to login. When logging in and then logging out again, the next time someone tries to log in instead of showing the CIS login screen. It is now once again showing the Microsoft login screen with no return option to the CIS login screen. This makes it not possible for other users to log into the application on that computer anymore without a Microsoft Entra account.

We have noticed it has to do something with cookies; we notice CIS adds a couple of cookies to the browser. Which if we then manually remove it works again, and the CIS login is once again shown. However, since these are HTTP-only and secure cookies, we cannot remove them with JavaScript.

Is there a way we can log out and remove these cookies automatically so CIS always shows its login screen when logging out?

We are using the following endpoint to logout: https://<Cloud Identity Services domain>/oauth2/logout

Beneath is a video that demonstrates this issue:

entry.title: How to have different welcome emails for Onboarding in IAS?
entry.content:
Hello,

We are implementing Onboarding for IAS, and we have the need to have the welcome email sent to the Onboardee at their creation be different depending on their Legal Entity.

I've created several email templates in IAS for the different Legal Entities, but I struggle for the conditions in the Target: the employee's data in the User entity are only populated with data once the "New Hire Data" step in Onboarding is completed, but we need to have the data since the Onboardee's creation.

Do you know how we can fetch the Onboardee's Legal Entity in IAS from the moment they are created, so as to use it in the conditions in the transformations to select the desired welcome email template?

 

Regards,

William BLANCO

entry.title: How to Add a Custom Button on IAS Login Page Redirecting to a Public URL
entry.content:
Hello,

We have an application that uses SAP IAS (Identity Authentication Service) for login. I need to add a custom button on the IAS login page that redirects users to a public URL. This button should work similarly to the "Not registered? Sign up here" link but should direct users to a different location.

I found this SAP Help blog: Configure User Access to Application (https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-identity-services/cloud-identity-services/configure-user-access-to-application?q=self+registration), but it focuses on self-registration within IAS. Instead, I need the button to redirect users to an external public URL.

How can I achieve this using IAS customization options? Any help would be greatly appreciated!

Thank you!

entry.title: Hi all "error": "IAS authentication is not 'ias'. It is: xsuaa. implementing the Build apps.
entry.content:
Hi all 

I am developing an sap build app mobile application. I had done all the Instances and Subscriptions to my project. when I go to sap build lobby and to open the project, getting error that 

 "error": "IAS authentication is not 'ias'. It is: xsuaa.

in btp cockpit security->users-> i found custom IAS tenent and checked all are ok but still getting the same error can any one help me to out.

entry.title: Custom Domain Question in SAP Cloud Identity Services
entry.content: If we create custom domain for IAS tenant, do we have to reconfigure SSO's of Applications and Corporate Identity Providers that are already configured in the tenant to use the custom domain URL's? Can the existing configurations continue to work with default *.accounts.ondemand.com url?

entry.title: Configuring SF SSO with IAS is really difficult for internal developer currently.
entry.content:
The manual SSO configuration in provisioning is deprecated. https://community.sap.com/t5/product-and-customer-updates/migration-to-sap-cloud-identity-authentication-ias-ips-innovation-alert-amp/ba-p/271407

However, automatic process doesn't work for internal developers. Currently there are 2 options.

1. via the SuccessFactors Upgrade Center.

2. via SPC process.

I tried both of them and an S-User is essential for the job. I'm an internal developer and I don't have the s user surely. It became really difficult for me to configure IAS for our Team's test instances in PROD/PREVIEW

entry.title: Step-by-Step Guide to Setting Up Joule with SAP SuccessFactors
entry.content:
SAP is advancing with AI innovations, and Joule - SAP's AI assistant - is making waves in how HR tasks are managed in SuccessFactors. Joule offers smart assistance for HR processes, making it easier for users to find quick answers, automate tasks, and simplify decision-making.

This blog offers a practical guide on implementing Joule in SAP SuccessFactors. The instructions are straightforward, suitable for both functional and technical teams to understand.

Before you Start Before starting the setup, make sure you have:

  1. An active SAP SuccessFactors License that includes access to SAP Joule.
  2. A global account in SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP).
  3. Integrated SuccessFactors with SAP Cloud Identity Services for secure user logins.

Step 1: Establish Trust Between SAP BTP and Identity Authentication This set up enables Joule to authenticate users via Identity Authentication.

Navigate:

  1. Log in to the SAP BTP Cockpit.
  2. Access Security → Trust Configuration.
  3. Establish Trust by adding your IAS tenant as a trusted identity provider.

Step 2: Configure User Attributes in IAS Joule uses basic user details such as first name, last name, and email for customization.

Navigate:

  1. Go to IAS Administration Console.
  2. Choose User Management → User Attributes.
  3. Define and map attributes like email, firstName, and lastName.

Step 3: Launch Joule Booster in SAP BTP Utilize SAP's automated "booster" to streamline Joule configuration.

Navigate:

  1. Access SAP BTP Cockpit with global admin privileges.
  2. Click on Boosters.
  3. Search for "Setting up Joule" booster and start.
  4. Follow the steps to select the subaccount and review entitlements.

Step 4: Add Trusted Domains in IAS for SuccessFactors Ensure secure communication between SuccessFactors and Joule.

Navigate:

  1. In IAS Admin Console, go to Applications & Resources → Applications.
  2. Choose SAP SuccessFactors application.
  3. In the Trust tab, add the domain used by your SuccessFactors system.

Step 5: Assign Joule Access via Permission Roles in SuccessFactors Grant necessary permissions to users for Joule access.

Navigate:

  1. Log in to SuccessFactors and access Admin Center.
  2. Manage Permission Roles.
  3. Select roles needing Joule access.
  4. Enable Joule Access checkbox under User Permissions.

Step 6: Test and Confirm Integration Verify that Joule is operating correctly after setup.

Test Steps:

  1. Log in to SAP SuccessFactors with correct permissions.
  2. Look for Joule icon or chat assistant.
  3. Interact with Joule by typing questions to confirm responses.

Conclusion By following these steps, you can integrate Joule into SAP SuccessFactors to enhance HR workflows with AI support. This integration boosts user engagement and productivity through intelligent insights and automation, whether for a full deployment or pilot group.

Review the official SAP docs here.

Joule

entry.title: IAS - SAC Role Maping
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I have the following scenario: Azure as the IdP, IAS as a proxy, and SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC).

My want is to implement IAS in a way that Azure AD groups are mapped to SAC teams, and then assign the corresponding SAC roles via SAML mapping.
For example: Azure group sac_adm → SAC team sac_adm → SAML mapping assigns the sac_adm role.

However, I have a few SAC teams where users within the same team need to have different roles in SAC.
How can this be achieved — having one team, but users within that team with different SAC roles?

 

Many Thanks

Best regards

entry.title: Azure /IAS User filtering
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I’m using Azure as IdP and IAS as a proxy.
In Azure, I have created groups which are then provisioned to cloud applications via IAS. In IPS I have set up a source system for Azure.

My requirement is to provision only specific groups and their users to IAS – for example, all groups whose names start with "SAP_" – without having to constantly modify the JSON transformation code.

To achieve this, I have configured the following parameters in the source system:

aad.group.filter = startsWith(displayName,'SAP_')

aad.user.filter.group.filter.combine = true

However, it seems that with these settings, all users in Azure are still being queried, which – as far as I understand – can lead to performance issues, especially in large environments.

My questions:

Is there a way to use the aad.user.filter parameter to ensure that only users who belong to the filtered groups (e.g., those with the prefix SAP_) are provisioned, without having to hardcode group names or regularly change the transformation code?

Is it possible to implement something like this:

aad.user.filter = users who belong to the groups defined in aad.group.filter

What is the recommended approach to efficiently and dynamically filter only the required groups and their members?

Thank you in advance for your support

 

Many Thanks

entry.title: SAP IAS - Can Application Custom Attributes Be Used in Provisioning to the Target System
entry.content:
Hi everyone, 

We are currently setting up user provisioning between SAP IAS as the source system and SAP Ariba as the target system, using SAP Identity Provisioning Service.

My question is about the Application Custom Attributes, which can be found in IAS under:
Users & Authorizations > User Management > [Select a User] > Applications.

Can these Application Custom Attributes (1–5) be used in the target system transformation mapping during provisioning?

We would like to use the values of these Application Custom Attributes in our IPS transformation mapping to populate fields in the target system (Ariba).

If it's not possible to use the Application Custom Attributes during the Provisioning, what are they used for?

Any clarification or best practice from the community would be highly appreciated!

 

Best regards 

Maurice
